text: Clicking "Execute!" on a Plan card should immediately disable the button and show a loading spinner in the button text to avoid accidentally clicking it multiple times and triggering multiple agents on the same task. The button should not become re-enabled / out of loading state until the agent has finished (whether successfully or with an error)

proposedTasks.title: Add loading state and double-click prevention to Execute! button
proposedTasks.description: When the user clicks "Execute!" on a Plan card, the button must immediately: (1) become disabled so no further clicks register, and (2) replace its label text with a spinner + loading indicator (e.g. "Executing…" with a spinner icon). The button must remain in this disabled/loading state until the orchestrator confirms the gating-task closure and agent dispatch have completed (success or error). On error, re-enable the button and show an inline error message so the user can retry. Acceptance criteria: - Clicking Execute! disables the button within the same event-loop tick (optimistic disable before any async call). - A visible spinner or animation appears inside the button while the operation is in flight. - No duplicate gating-task closures or duplicate agent spawns can result from rapid clicks. - On success the button transitions to its post-execution state (e.g. hidden or replaced with status). - On failure the button re-enables with an error toast/message and the plan remains in its pre-execution state.

taskTitles: Add loading state and double-click prevention to Execute! button
